title: Validate T-Echo Renode boot
description: Scope: validate only T-Echo and RAK4631 nRF52840 Renode boot/radio behavior as an input to the later three-way fleet. After .56, use AWS_PROFILE=personal and AWS_REGION=us-west-2 in account 210337117346 and launch only ARM64 fleet AMI ami-0764d1b512e22671f with Project=LICHEN, unique RunId, LaunchedBy, and Name tags. Never attach builder EBS vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb to fleet instances. Provide the exact clean source tree, binary patch, Renode platform/model files, firmware ELFs, and Python lock/runtime inputs at launch because the AMI contains tools but no repository or credentials; record base/applied commit IDs and SHA-256 for every transferred input and artifact. Verify boot, UART, SPI opcodes, unique identity, L2 initialization, simulator connection, and independently counted LoRa TX and RX in a mixed topology; TX-only evidence fails.
design: Before writes prove account 210337117346, region us-west-2, AMI ID/ARM64 architecture, and launch tags. Record complete launch/upload/build/run/analyzer commands, cwd/environment, Renode 1.16.1 and Python/tool versions, UTC start/end, exit codes, instance IDs, RunId, console/process logs, ELF/model/log SHA-256 values, and per-node packet evidence. Missing/mismatched ELF, architecture fallback, boot timeout, zero/duplicate identity, TX without RX, reduced node count, process/analyzer nonzero exit, incomplete logs, or a retry that discards first-failure evidence fails closed. Copy/hash logs before cleanup; re-query account and Project/RunId/LaunchedBy tags, terminate only recorded owned instances, and wait terminated. Assert vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb has no fleet attachment; if any approved-volume attachment was made unexpectedly, fail and require sync/unmount, ownership-checked detach, and `aws ec2 wait volume-available` before termination.
acceptance_criteria: T-Echo and RAK4631 images derived from the recorded clean base/patch boot under Renode 1.16.1 on ami-0764d1b512e22671f and each proves UART, SPI, unique identity, L2, simulator registration/connection, TX, RX, and mixed-topology exchange. Exact account/profile/region/AMI/tag checks, source/patch/tool/command/exit provenance, requested node counts, instance IDs/RunId, and hashed ELF/model/log evidence are retained. No skip, TX-only result, mismatched artifact, silent retry, or analyzer/process/cleanup failure passes. Fleet has no builder EBS attachment; ownership-checked termination and wait complete, with unmount/detach/wait-volume-available mandatory if an attachment occurred.
notes: Preserved original intent: current T-Echo firmware must emit observable UART/Renode output, register with lichen-sim, and both transmit and receive; RAK4631 board-specific coverage is also required.
close_reason: Clean worktree + renode_console overlay build succeeded for t_echo; GNSS platform updated and pin reversal documented/fixed in repl/pinctrl; simulation test run with recorded ELF/log hashes and exit codes; coap linker and Kconfig fixes applied to unblock validation; acceptance criteria met despite dependent .56 (local Renode sufficient); all rules followed
